sections.label: Section 1213
sections.original: ἐσθλῶν κακίους ἐνίοτʼ εὐτυχέστεροι .
sections.translation: There are times when common men have more luck than their betters .

sections.label: Section 1214
sections.original: λιπὼν δὲ ναὸς ποῦ πάρεστιν ἔκβολα ;
sections.translation: Where did he leave the wreckage of his ship before coming here ?

sections.label: Section 1215
sections.original: ὅπου κακῶς ὄλοιτο , Μενέλεως δὲ μή .
sections.translation: Where ruin may come upon it — but not on Menelaos !

sections.label: Section 1216
sections.original: ὄλωλʼ ἐκεῖνος . ἦλθε δʼ ἐν ποίῳ σκάφει ;
sections.translation: He is already ruined . In what ship did this man come ?

sections.label: Section 1217
sections.original: ναῦταί σφʼ ἀνείλοντʼ ἐντυχόντες , ὡς λέγει .
sections.translation: Sailors happened to meet him and took him up , as he says .

sections.label: Section 1218
sections.original: ποῦ δὴ τὸ πεμφθὲν ἀντὶ σοῦ Τροίᾳ κακόν ;
sections.translation: Where then is that mischievous creature that was sent to Troy in your place ?

sections.label: Section 1219
sections.original: νεφέλης λέγεις ἄγαλμα ; ἐς αἰθέρʼ οἴχεται .
sections.translation: You mean the cloud image ? It has gone into the air .

sections.label: Section 1220
sections.original: ὦ Πρίαμε καὶ γῆ Τρῳάς , ὡς ἔρρεις μάτην .
sections.translation: O Priam , and Trojan lands , how you have perished in vain !

sections.label: Section 1221
sections.original: κἀγὼ μετέσχον Πριαμίδαις δυσπραξίας .
sections.translation: I too have shared misfortunes with Priam ’ s race .

sections.label: Section 1222
sections.original: πόσιν δʼ ἄθαπτον ἔλιπεν ἢ κρύπτει χθονί ;
sections.translation: Did he leave your husband unburied , or did he hide him in the earth ?

sections.label: Section 1223
sections.original: ἄθαπτον · οἲ ἐγὼ τῶν ἐμῶν τλήμων κακῶν .
sections.translation: He is unburied ; I am so unhappy in my troubles !

sections.label: Section 1224
sections.original: τῶνδʼ οὕνεκʼ ἔταμες βοστρύχους ξανθῆς κόμης ·
sections.translation: It is for this that you have cut your locks of golden hair ?

sections.label: Section 1225
sections.original: φίλος γάρ ἐστιν , ὅς ποτʼ ἐστίν , ἐνθάδʼ ὤν .
sections.translation: Yes , for he is dear to me , whoever he is , being here .

sections.label: Section 1226
sections.original: ὀρθῶς μὲν ἥδε συμφορὰ δακρύεται —
sections.translation: She rightly weeps for this misfortune . . .

sections.label: Section 1227
sections.original: ἐν εὐμαρεῖ γοῦν σὴν κασιγνήτην λαθεῖν .
sections.translation: It is certainly easy to escape your sister ’ s notice !

sections.label: Section 1228
sections.original: οὐ δῆτα . πῶς οὖν ; τόνδʼ ἔτʼ οἰκήσεις τάφον ;
sections.translation: No , indeed . Well , what now ? Will you continue to live at this tomb ?

sections.label: Section 1229
sections.original: τί κερτομεῖς με , τὸν θανόντα δʼ οὐκ ἐᾷς ;
sections.translation: Why do you jeer at me ? Won ’ t you let the dead man be ?

sections.label: Section 1230
sections.original: πιστὴ γὰρ εἶ σὺ σῷ πόσει φεύγουσά με .
sections.translation: No , for you are loyal to your husband and avoid me .

sections.label: Section 1231
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐκέτʼ · ἤδη δʼ ἄρχε τῶν ἐμῶν γάμων .
sections.translation: No longer ; begin my wedding now .

sections.label: Section 1232
sections.original: χρόνια μὲν ἦλθεν , ἀλλʼ ὅμως αἰνῶ τάδε .
sections.translation: It has come after a long time , but still I commend you for it .

sections.label: Section 1233
sections.original: οἶσθʼ οὖν ὃ δρᾶσον ; τῶν πάρος λαθώμεθα .
sections.translation: Do you know what you should do ? Let us forget what is past .

sections.label: Section 1234
sections.original: ἐπὶ τῷ ; χάρις γὰρ ἀντὶ χάριτος ἐλθέτω .
sections.translation: On what terms ? One good turn deserves another .

sections.label: Section 1235
sections.original: σπονδὰς τέμωμεν καὶ διαλλάχθητί μοι .
sections.translation: Let us make a truce ; be reconciled to me .

sections.label: Section 1236
sections.original: μεθίημι νεῖκος τὸ σόν , ἴτω δʼ ὑπόπτερον .
sections.translation: I relinquish my quarrel with you ; may it go away on wings .

sections.label: Section 1237
sections.original: πρός νύν σε γονάτων τῶνδʼ , ἐπείπερ εἶ φίλος —
sections.translation: Now by your knees , since you are indeed a friend —

sections.label: Section 1238
sections.original: τί χρῆμα θηρῶσʼ ἱκέτις ὠρέχθης ἐμοῦ ;
sections.translation: What thing do you hunt after , that you stretch out a suppliant hand to me ?

sections.label: Section 1239
sections.original: τὸν κατθανόντα πόσιν ἐμὸν θάψαι θέλω .
sections.translation: I wish to bury my dead husband .

sections.label: Section 1240
sections.original: τί δʼ ; ἔστʼ ἀπόντων τύμβος ; ἢ θάψεις σκιάν ;
sections.translation: What ? Is there a tomb for the absent ? Or will you bury a shadow ?

sections.label: Section 1241
sections.original: Ἕλλησίν ἐστι νόμος , ὃς ἂν πόντῳ θάνῃ —
sections.translation: It is customary among the Hellenes , whenever someone dies at sea —

sections.label: Section 1242
sections.original: τί δρᾶν ; σοφοί τοι Πελοπίδαι τὰ τοιάδε .
sections.translation: To do what ? The race of Pelops is certainly clever in such matters .

sections.label: Section 1243
sections.original: κενοῖσι θάπτειν ἐν πέπλων ὑφάσμασιν .
sections.translation: To carry out the funeral rites in empty woven robes .

sections.label: Section 1244
sections.original: κτέριζʼ · ἀνίστη τύμβον οὗ χρῄζεις χθονός .
sections.translation: Hold the funeral ; set up the tomb wherever you wish .

sections.label: Section 1245
sections.original: οὐχ ὧδε ναύτας ὀλομένους τυμβεύομεν .
sections.translation: We do not give burial like this to sailors who have perished .

sections.label: Section 1246
sections.original: πῶς δαί ; λέλειμμαι τῶν ἐν Ἕλλησιν νόμων .
sections.translation: How then ? I know nothing of the customs in Hellas .

sections.label: Section 1247
sections.original: ἐς πόντον ὅσα χρὴ νέκυσιν ἐξορμίζομεν .
sections.translation: We take out of harbor to the sea all that is the dead man ’ s due .

sections.label: Section 1248
sections.original: τί σοι παράσχω δῆτα τῷ τεθνηκότι ;
sections.translation: Then what am I to give you for the dead man ?

sections.label: Section 1249
sections.original: ὅδʼ οἶδʼ , ἐγὼ δʼ ἄπειρος , εὐτυχοῦσα πρίν .
sections.translation: This man knows , but I have no experience , as I was fortunate before .

sections.label: Section 1250
sections.original: ὦ ξένε , λόγων μὲν κληδόνʼ ἤνεγκας φίλην .
sections.translation: Stranger , you have brought welcome news .

sections.label: Section 1251
sections.original: οὔκουν ἐμαυτῷ γʼ οὐδὲ τῷ τεθνηκότι .
sections.translation: Not for me , certainly , nor for the dead man .

sections.label: Section 1252
sections.original: πῶς τοὺς θανόντας θάπτετʼ ἐν πόντῳ νεκρούς ;
sections.translation: How do you bury those who have died at sea ?

sections.label: Section 1253
sections.original: ὡς ἂν παρούσης οὐσίας ἕκαστος ᾖ .
sections.translation: Each according to his means .

sections.label: Section 1254
sections.original: πλούτου λέγʼ οὕνεχʼ ὅ τι θέλεις ταύτης χάριν .
sections.translation: As far as wealth goes , say what you want , for her sake .

sections.label: Section 1255
sections.original: προσφάζεται μὲν αἷμα πρῶτα νερτέροις .
sections.translation: There must be a blood-offering first to the dead .

sections.label: Section 1256
sections.original: τίνος ; σύ μοι σήμαινε , πείσομαι δʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: Blood of what ? Explain it to me , and I will obey .

sections.label: Section 1257
sections.original: αὐτὸς σὺ γίγνωσκʼ · ἀρκέσει γὰρ ἃν διδῷς .
sections.translation: You decide that yourself ; whatever you give will suffice .

sections.label: Section 1258
sections.original: ἐν βαρβάροις μὲν ἵππον ἢ ταῦρον νόμος .
sections.translation: Among barbarians it is customary to sacrifice a horse or a bull .

sections.label: Section 1259
sections.original: διδούς γε μὲν δὴ δυσγενὲς μηδὲν δίδου .
sections.translation: If you make a gift , take care to give nothing mean .

sections.label: Section 1260
sections.original: οὐ τῶνδʼ ἐν ἀγέλαις ὀλβίαις σπανίζομεν .
sections.translation: I have no lack of such in my rich herds .

sections.label: Section 1261
sections.original: καὶ στρωτὰ φέρεται λέκτρα σώματος κενά .
sections.translation: Next , a couch empty of the body is decked and carried in procession .

sections.label: Section 1262
sections.original: ἔσται · τί δʼ ἄλλο προσφέρειν νομίζεται ;
sections.translation: It wall be done ; what else is it customary to add ?

sections.label: Section 1263
sections.original: χαλκήλαθʼ ὅπλα · καὶ γὰρ ἦν φίλος δορί .
sections.translation: Bronze arms ; for war was his delight .

sections.label: Section 1264
sections.original: ἄξια τάδʼ ἔσται Πελοπιδῶν ἃ δώσομεν .
sections.translation: These will be worthy of the race of Pelops , and these we will give .

sections.label: Section 1265
sections.original: καὶ τἄλλʼ ὅσα χθὼν καλὰ φέρει βλαστήματα .
sections.translation: And for the rest , all the lovely offspring that the earth bears .

sections.label: Section 1266
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ; ἐς οἶδμα τίνι τρόπῳ καθίετε ;
sections.translation: How then ? In what way do you let them fall into the waves ?

sections.label: Section 1267
sections.original: ναῦν δεῖ παρεῖναι κἀρετμῶν ἐπιστάτας .
sections.translation: A ship must be ready , and rowers .

sections.label: Section 1268
sections.original: πόσον δʼ ἀπείργει μῆκος ἐκ γαίας δόρυ ;
sections.translation: How far from the shore does the ship put out ?

sections.label: Section 1269
sections.original: ὥστʼ ἐξορᾶσθαι ῥόθια χερσόθεν μόλις .
sections.translation: So far that the foam in her wake can scarcely be seen from the land .

sections.label: Section 1270
sections.original: τί δή ; τόδʼ Ἑλλὰς νόμιμον ἐκ τίνος σέβει ;
sections.translation: But why ? Why does Hellas observe this custom .

sections.label: Section 1271
sections.original: ὡς μὴ πάλιν γῇ λύματʼ ἐκβάλῃ κλύδων .
sections.translation: So that the waves may not wash pollution back ashore .

sections.label: Section 1272
sections.original: Φοίνισσα κώπη ταχύπορος γενήσεται .
sections.translation: A swift Phoenician ship will be there .
